test.name: Evidence-quality question should route to assertion-classification
test.description: User asks whether the informant on src_004 (James Brown, son-in-law) makes the father's-name entry primary or secondary information. That is an evidence-quality question for assertion-classification. The citation skill corrects terminology if it appears in a citation string but does not evaluate information weight.
test.xfail_reason: Flaky under single-run variance (runs_per_test=1). Correct behavior is to route this evidence-quality question to assertion-classification (or decline in text naming it). June runs routed correctly and passed (skills_invoked: [assertion-classification]); July runs answered the primary/secondary question inline (skills_invoked: []) and failed. Same model/harness_version/judge_prompt_hash/descriptions across both, so this is not caused by any SKILL.md body change (routing is description-driven and no description changed). Remove this marker when routing stabilizes or the description optimizer resolves it.

input.user_message: Is the informant on src_004 primary or secondary for the father's name?

negative.explanation: Assessing whether information on a record is primary or secondary based on informant proximity is assertion-classification's job. The citation skill corrects source-vs-information terminology when it appears in a citation string it is formatting, but does not evaluate the weight or quality of information on a source.

judge_context: May gently note the terminology distinction (primary/secondary applies to information, not sources) but must still redirect
Should NOT attempt to answer the primary/secondary question itself
